headline: US versus China: a test of strength
summary: Gideon Rachman talks to Michael Froman, president of the Council on Foreign Relations and former US trade representative
body_text:
This is an audio transcript of the Rachman Review podcast episode: ‘US versus China: a test of strength’

[AI SHIFT AD PLAYING]

Gideon RachmanHello and welcome to the Rachman Review. I’m Gideon Rachman, chief foreign affairs commentator of the Financial Times. This week’s podcast is about China, America and international trade. My guest is Michael Froman, president of the Council on Foreign Relations in New York, and a former US trade representative.

So in the struggle over trade and tariffs between America and China, which country has the stronger hand?

Donald Trump voice clipI am here on a mission of friendship and goodwill, and to deepen our ties of commerce, to strengthen our common security, and to really promote strongly stability, prosperity, and peace for all of the countries in this room and long beyond this room.

Gideon RachmanDonald Trump’s been in Asia this week. He visited the Asean summit in Malaysia, then went on to Japan where he met the country’s new prime minister
